title: Orchestrator: run tracking + event emission
analysis: PipelineOrchestrator.run() in autopilot/orchestrator.py currently executes stages in topological order, logging progress and returning a dict of StageResult. Task 35 (dependency) already added pipeline_runs and pipeline_events tables plus full CRUD to CatalogDB (db.insert_run, db.update_run, db.insert_event, etc.). This task instruments run() to: (1) create a pipeline_runs record at start via db.insert_run with uuid4 run_id, (2) add a _emit_event helper that calls db.insert_event with JSON payload, (3) emit stage_started/stage_completed/stage_error events at each stage transition with current_stage updates on the run, (4) finalize the run at end with status completed|failed, finished_at, wall_clock_seconds, (5) track budget_remaining_seconds. Existing tests use MagicMock for db, so all new db.insert_run/insert_event/update_run calls are silently accepted — no existing test breakage. New tests will assert those mock calls happened with correct args. An integration test with real in-memory CatalogDB verifies actual DB records.

steps.description: Write TestRunTrackingInit class with tests: (1) test_run_creates_pipeline_run_record — after run() with mock stages, db.insert_run called once with 32-char hex run_id, started_at ISO string, status='running', config_snapshot is a string; (2) test_run_stores_run_id_on_self — after run(), orch._run_id is a 32-char hex string; (3) test_run_passes_budget_to_insert_run — when budget_seconds=3600, insert_run called with budget_remaining_seconds=3600

Add imports (uuid4, datetime, timezone) to orchestrator.py. At start of run(), generate run_id=uuid4().hex, started_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(), store self._run_id=run_id and self._db=db, call db.insert_run(run_id, started_at=started_at, config_snapshot=str(config), status='running', budget_remaining_seconds=self.budget_seconds)

Write TestEmitEvent class with tests: (1) test_emit_event_calls_insert_event — set orch._db to a MagicMock, call _emit_event('test_event', stage='INGEST'), verify db.insert_event called with event_type='test_event', stage='INGEST'; (2) test_emit_event_serializes_payload_to_json — call with payload={'key': 'val'}, verify payload_json='{"key": "val"}'; (3) test_emit_event_none_payload — call without payload, verify payload_json=None; (4) test_emit_event_logs_at_debug_level — verify debug log emitted

Add _emit_event(self, event_type, stage=None, job_id=None, payload=None) method to PipelineOrchestrator. Calls self._db.insert_event(event_type, stage=stage, job_id=job_id, payload_json=json.dumps(payload) if payload is not None else None). Logs at debug level.

Write TestStageTransitionEvents class with tests: (1) test_stage_started_event_emitted_for_each_stage — run with all mock stages, verify db.insert_event called with event_type='stage_started' and correct stage name for each of the 9 stages; (2) test_stage_completed_event_emitted_with_elapsed — verify 'stage_completed' events include payload with 'elapsed' key; (3) test_stage_error_event_emitted — make one stage raise, verify 'stage_error' event with error message in payload; (4) test_current_stage_updated_via_update_run — verify db.update_run called with current_stage=name before each stage runs

In run() stage loop, before calling stage.func: emit 'stage_started' event and call db.update_run(run_id, current_stage=stage_name). After successful stage: emit 'stage_completed' event with payload={'elapsed': elapsed}. In exception handler: emit 'stage_error' event with payload={'error': str(exc)}.

Write TestRunFinalization class with tests: (1) test_run_completed_status_on_success — when all stages pass, db.update_run called with status='completed', finished_at (ISO string), wall_clock_seconds (float >= 0); (2) test_run_failed_status_on_error — when a stage errors, final update_run has status='failed'; (3) test_run_completed_event_emitted — 'run_completed' event with payload={'duration': float}; (4) test_run_failed_event_emitted — 'run_failed' event emitted when pipeline has errors

After the stage loop and summary logging in run(), determine status='failed' if errored_stages else 'completed'. Call db.update_run(run_id, finished_at=datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(), status=status, wall_clock_seconds=total_elapsed). Emit 'run_completed' or 'run_failed' event with payload={'duration': total_elapsed}.

Write TestBudgetRemainingTracking class with tests: (1) test_budget_remaining_written_after_each_stage — with budget_seconds=3600, verify db.update_run called with budget_remaining_seconds after each stage; (2) test_no_budget_remaining_when_no_budget — with budget_seconds=None, verify update_run not called with budget_remaining_seconds for per-stage updates (only called for current_stage and finalization)

In the progress reporting section of run() (after each stage), if budget_seconds is not None: compute remaining = budget_seconds - cumulative, call db.update_run(run_id, budget_remaining_seconds=remaining).

Write TestRunTrackingIntegration class using real in-memory CatalogDB (catalog_db fixture): (1) test_full_run_creates_db_records — run orchestrator with mock stage funcs and real CatalogDB, verify pipeline_runs row exists with correct status='completed' and wall_clock_seconds > 0; (2) test_full_run_creates_events — verify pipeline_events table has stage_started + stage_completed events for each stage plus run_completed; (3) test_dry_run_still_creates_run_record — dry_run=True still creates pipeline_runs record
Run full test suite (pytest tests/test_orchestrator.py -x) to verify all existing + new tests pass. Fix any issues.

design_decisions.decision: Store self._db and self._run_id as instance attributes during run()
design_decisions.rationale: _emit_event needs access to db and the helper signature from the task spec (_emit_event(event_type, stage, job_id, payload)) doesn't take db as a parameter. Storing on self is the cleanest approach since run() is never called concurrently on the same instance.

design_decisions.decision: Use str(config) for config_snapshot rather than yaml.dump
design_decisions.rationale: AutopilotConfig is a dataclass, so str() gives a readable representation. yaml.dump would require the config to be serializable to YAML which isn't guaranteed. The task suggests 'yaml.dump(config) or str(config)' — str() is always safe.

design_decisions.decision: Emit events even in dry_run mode for run start/end but not for stage transitions
design_decisions.rationale: In dry_run, stages aren't actually executed (they're logged as DRY-RUN), so stage_started/stage_completed don't apply. However, the run itself is created and finalized so we can track dry runs. The stage loop's dry_run branch continues before reaching the event emission code.
Consistent with the DB's existing datetime('now') convention, produces ISO 8601 format that's human-readable and sortable. UTC avoids timezone ambiguity.

design_decisions.decision: _emit_event logs at DEBUG level rather than INFO
design_decisions.rationale: Events are stored in the DB for the web console to query. Debug-level logging avoids cluttering the console output which already has INFO-level RUNNING/DONE logs. This also prevents existing log-assertion tests from breaking.

design_decisions.decision: Track budget_remaining_seconds only when budget_seconds is set
design_decisions.rationale: When budget_seconds is None, budget_remaining_seconds is meaningless. Avoids writing None - cumulative which would error. Matches existing pattern where budget logging is conditional on self.budget_seconds being truthy.

reuse.how: Called at start of run() to create the pipeline_runs record with run_id, started_at, config_snapshot, status, budget_remaining_seconds
Called to update current_stage at each transition, budget_remaining_seconds after each stage, and finalize with finished_at/status/wall_clock_seconds at end
Called by _emit_event helper to insert stage_started, stage_completed, stage_error, run_completed, run_failed events

reuse.what: Existing test patterns with MagicMock for db and config
reuse.how: New test classes follow same pattern: create PipelineOrchestrator, replace stage funcs with MagicMock, run with MagicMock config/db, assert on db mock calls
Used in integration tests to verify actual DB records are created with correct values
Used in _emit_event to serialize payload dict to JSON string for insert_event's payload_json parameter
